Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Iran warns US tech firms cold become targets as war expands 18 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Physicist Astrid Eichhorn is a leader in the field of asymptotic safety 93 upvotes, 13 comments
  3. Fungal Electronics (2021) 43 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. I'm glad the Anthropic fight is happening now 24 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. The mechanics of autonomous software translation 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Temporal: A nine-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 373 upvotes, 131 comments
  7. Show HN: Open-source browser for AI agents 77 upvotes, 21 comments
  8. Show HN: I built an ISP infrastructure emulator from scratch with a custom vBNG 37 upvotes, 8 comments
  9. Show HN: I built a tool that watches webpages and exposes changes as RSS 84 upvotes, 26 comments
  10. How we hacked McKinsey's AI platform 332 upvotes, 133 comments
  11. The dead Internet is not a theory anymore 96 upvotes, 32 comments
  12. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans. 1029 upvotes, 472 comments
  13. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 280 upvotes, 116 comments
  14. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 196 upvotes, 78 comments
  15. BitNet: 100B Param 1-Bit model for local CPUs 265 upvotes, 130 comments
  16. Launch HN: Sentrial (YC W26) – Catch AI agent failures before your users do 20 upvotes, 6 comments
  17. Show HN: Satellite imagery object detection using text prompts 26 upvotes, 9 comments
  18. Show HN: Klaus – OpenClaw on a VM, batteries included 88 upvotes, 52 comments
  19. Show HN: Vanilla JavaScript refinery simulator built to explain job to my kids 66 upvotes, 37 comments
  20. Entities enabling scientific fraud at scale (2025) 229 upvotes, 158 comments
  21. Google closes deal to acquire Wiz 145 upvotes, 95 comments
  22. Personal Computer by Perplexity 17 upvotes, 6 comments
  23. 5,200 holes carved into a Peruvian mountain left by an ancient economy 57 upvotes, 33 comments
  24. Lego's 0.002mm specification and its implications for manufacturing (2025) 316 upvotes, 261 comments
  25. Launch HN: Prism (YC X25) – Workspace and API to generate and edit videos 25 upvotes, 14 comments
  26. We Were Right About Havana Syndrome 42 upvotes, 28 comments
  27. What Is a Tort? 11 upvotes, 6 comments
  28. I Was Interviewed by an AI Bot for a Job 29 upvotes, 28 comments
  29. The MacBook Neo 247 upvotes, 440 comments
  30. Swiss e-voting pilot can't count 2,048 ballots after decryption failure 107 upvotes, 237 comments

Last generated 5:00am, 12 March 2026. Source here.
